articles.title: THE INTEGRATIONS RACE: HOW AI PRODUCTS ARE BUILDING MOATS
articles.summary: Aside from adapting to the newest LLMs coming out, the biggest race between AI products today, is integrations.

articles.title: READ "WHY AI COMPANIES ARE RACING TO BUILD INTEGRATIONS"
APPLE INNOVATION AND EXECUTION
articles.summary: Apple faces ongoing skepticism about its innovation as its last major products stagnate, services rely on existing customers, and recent struggles—like the Vision Pro's lukewarm reception and delayed AI-driven Siri update—raise concerns about its ability to deliver groundbreaking technology.

articles.title: 10 PREDICTIONS FOR THE FUTURE OF THE FINANCE TECH STACK
articles.summary: This report highlights a fragmented finance tech landscape with trends in AI-driven billing, AR consolidation, unified expense management, SMB procurement, early-stage sales compensation, and real-time forecasting.

articles.title: PRODUCT MANAGERS WHO BUILD TOGETHER, WIN TOGETHER
WHY STAKEHOLDERS DON'T VIBE WITH USER RESEARCH
articles.summary: Many stakeholders resist user research due to a lack of understanding, control, time, or clear incentives, often prioritizing quick design changes over informed decisions. To overcome this, teams can educate stakeholders, make a strong case for research, leverage AI for efficiency, use free research methods, and adopt a Lean UX approach to ensure user needs shape better products.

articles.title: AVOID THE RESUME PILE: THREE WAYS TO STAND OUT IN THE PRODUCT OPS JOB MARKET
articles.summary: The job market for product operations roles is highly competitive, with many job seekers struggling to land positions despite extensive experience. To stand out, candidates should highlight their product ops superpowers, showcase transferable skills through compelling storytelling, and actively network within industry communities to build relationships that lead to job opportunities.

articles.title: 15 PRODUCT ADOPTION METRICS: HOW TO MEASURE PRODUCT ADOPTION IN 2025
articles.summary: Tracking product adoption metrics helps teams understand user behavior, refine onboarding, and drive long-term engagement. By aligning key metrics with the user journey (covering acquisition, activation, engagement, and retention), companies can identify friction points, improve adoption, and ensure sustained success.

articles.title: DRIVER + NAVIGATOR
articles.summary: The Driver + Navigator model simplifies roles and responsibilities by designating Drivers, who lead and make decisions, and Navigators, who provide input and collaborate without final authority. This flexible approach fosters cross-functional teamwork, avoids handoffs, and enhances clarity in decision-making across various workflows, from meetings to product development stages.

articles.title: THE MARKETING EFFICIENCY & ATTRIBUTION PLAYBOOK: WHAT TODAY'S CMOS ARE TRACKING
articles.summary: B2B marketing attribution remains complex, with CMOs prioritizing pipeline and ARR over efficiency metrics like cost per revenue. Larger companies adopt multi-touch attribution and blended models for better investment insights, while the future of marketing measurement lies in combining attribution models with deeper cost analysis to justify budget allocations more accurately.

articles.title: HOW TO MEASURE VALUE?
articles.summary: Measuring value isn't about outputs—it's about outcomes.

articles.title: THE 3 BIGGEST MISTAKES LEADERS MAKE WHEN HIRING THEIR FIRST PMS
articles.summary: Founders should hire a PM who fits their immediate needs, prioritizing execution and adaptability over seniority or perfect credentials.
